economic_notes: Nation receives 57% of revenue from ISC, FNIHB, Province of Ontario and other funding agencies. Involved in forestry operations (Island Falls Forestry LP), hydro-electric power (Coral Rapids Power Corporation - one-third interest in Peter Sutherland Sr. Generating Station LP), catering services (Weekashin Domco LP). OFNLP gaming revenue distributions restricted to community development, health, education, cultural and economic development. Four entities excluded from consolidation due to unavailable financial information: Pahquataskkahmekook LP, First Nation Yellow Falls LP (50%), Kisis Aski Energy Inc. (50%), SE5 2013 LP (50.01%).
notes: Audit is qualified for three reasons: (1) financial information unavailable for several limited partnerships (Pahquataskkahmekook LP, First Nation Yellow Falls LP, Kisis Aski Energy Inc., SE5 2013 LP) preventing classification under PSAS; (2) insufficient audit evidence for prior year accounts receivable and revenues of Apitisawin LP; (3) no budget figures available as required by PSAS. Emphasis of matter regarding restatement of 2019 comparative figures due to error correction related to Island Falls Forestry GBE reporting. ISC funding calculated as Fixed ($1,866,922) + Flexible ($121,624) + NFR Grant ($1,202,531) = $3,191,077 (Note: ISC Grant and Set funding were $0 in 2020). Total government transfers include ISC, CMHC, FNIHB, Mushkegowuk Council, NAN, OFNLP, OPG, Province of Ontario. OSR includes Kirkland Lake Gold, earnings from FN business entities, management fees, rental income, other revenue, net of repayment of funding and deferred revenue movements. COVID-19 noted as causing ISC to temporarily convert set funded programs to fixed funding.
